text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-tooltip repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: react-tooltip attaches delegated event listeners at the document level and, for each incoming event, resolves which anchor element (if any) the event relates to. That anchor-resolution logic assumes the event's `target` is always an element node and reads element-only properties/methods off it (such as attribute lookups or `closest(...)`). But some events have a `target` that is not an element at all — for example a `mouseover` dispatched with the `document` itself as its target. When that happens, the code throws, which breaks the library's delegated event handling. The correct behavior is to safely ignore event targets that are not element nodes: such an event should be a no-op (no thrown exception, and no tooltip incorrectly attached). Fix the library source so that non-element event targets are handled gracefully. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: no root access is needed. Dependencies are already installed via `npm install -g yarn@1.22.22 && yarn install --ignore-scripts --ignore-engines`. Run tests with Jest, e.g. `node_modules/.bin/jest src/test/tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js`. The event-delegation and anchor-resolution logic lives under `src/components/Tooltip/` and `src/utils/`. Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

message: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.

text: The failure is in `resolveAnchorElementRef`: it casts `event.target` to `HTMLElement` and only checks connectivity, so `document` passes through to `matches`, `closest`, or `contains`. I’m checking the project’s existing DOM type-guard conventions before applying a narrowly scoped element-node guard.

text: The source fix is now in place: anchor resolution returns `null` unless the event target is an actual `Element`, so `Document`, text nodes, and other non-element targets become no-ops. I’m running the focused interaction suite and TypeScript validation now.

text: Focused interaction tests pass (9/9), and TypeScript validation passes. The full Jest run had one unrelated failure in the existing imperative zero-content test (175/176 tests passed); the failure showed the tooltip rendered correctly but `isOpen` remained false. I’m doing a final diff/whitespace check and confirming only the intended source change is attributable to this fix.

text:
Fixed `src/components/Tooltip/use-tooltip-events.tsx` to ignore non-element event targets before anchor resolution.

Validation:

- Focused Jest suite: 9/9 passed
- TypeScript check: passed
- Full Jest: 175/176 passed; one unrelated existing imperative tooltip test failed

`yarn.lock` was already modified and was left untouched.
